signals.reasoning: BTC is still stabilizing in a fragile post-flush range while the board remains under active Hormuz-related veto. There is no clean continuation short or trustworthy reversal trigger here, so staying flat is the higher-quality choice.

ETH is showing the same weak stabilization pattern as BTC, with modest fresh-shorts-pressing mechanics but no real breakdown acceleration. Under a live geopolitical veto, that is not enough edge for a new trade.

SOL still has some squeeze fuel from negative funding and prior long-liquidation behavior, but the actual chart is just a muted bounce inside damaged structure. In this event regime, it remains watch-only rather than actionable.

STABLE screens bullish mechanically because funding is deeply negative and price is still up sharply, but the move is already late and highly event-sensitive. This is the kind of extended squeeze name that can reverse hard on headline risk, so I would not chase it under veto.

AAVE has cleaner liquidity than most alts on this sheet, but the current move already looks like a bounce in a still-fragile market rather than a fresh low-risk continuation long. That keeps it in watch territory, not active trade territory.
GOAT is already deep into a liquidation-style flush, which makes fresh shorts late and fresh longs too speculative. The veto environment lowers tolerance for this kind of unstable post-damage setup.
FTT remains too thin and too spread-out to trust. Even if the mechanical bias leans one way or the other, the liquidity quality is not acceptable for a fresh signal.
REZ is only showing a small rebound after weakness, with short-covering characteristics rather than a clean new trend. That leaves no compelling edge for either side.
ENA is bouncing on short-covering behavior, but not in a way that creates a high-conviction continuation long. With macro still RED and the veto active, it does not clear the bar for deployment.

0G again has the strongest raw squeeze metrics on the sheet, but it is extremely extended, thin, and vulnerable to violent reversal. Under an active geopolitical veto, that is a watchlist setup, not a responsible fresh entry.
GRASS has bounced but still lacks the combination of liquidity, trend clarity, and event insulation needed for a real signal. It stays on the watchlist rather than the trade list.
